extraction.one_line_summary: MATTHEWS Michael won bunch sprint after EVENEPOEL Remco took intermediate bonus seconds over ROGLIČ Primož, with MCNULTY Brandon surprisingly dropped on final climb.
tactical_patterns: EVENEPOEL Remco actively contested intermediate sprint bonifications rather than leaving them to pure sprinters, gaining three seconds over ROGLIČ.
Jayco-AlUla successfully distanced pure sprinters including MILAN Jonathan on penultimate climb despite wet conditions favoring heavy engines.
PINOT Thibaut attacked twice specifically for KOM points rather than stage victory, revealing classification targeting over stage ambitions.
MCNULTY Brandon dropped on relatively minor final climb suggests UAE overextended him in opening stages defending yellow jersey.

rider_signals.MATTHEWS Michael: positive: First Giro stage win since 2015 after team successfully filtered sprint field on wet climbs
rider_signals.MCNULTY Brandon: negative: Unexpectedly dropped on final climb of 213km stage, potential early GC form concerns for UAE
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: positive: Contested intermediate sprint for bonifications despite holding pink, showing aggressive time defense approach
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: Recovered from being dropped on climb to contest sprint for second, exceptional diesel engine recovery
rider_signals.PINOT Thibaut: neutral: Targeted KOM points exclusively with repeated attacks, first grand tour classification lead in years
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Caught in wet descent crash, required significant effort to rejoin suggesting vulnerable positioning in rain
